habitat: Evergreen forests; lowlands; and lower hill-slopes; up to 1000 m
story: Explore the fascinating world of the Wallace Hawk Eagle (Nisaetus nanus). Classified under the Accipitridae family, this bird predominantly inhabits Evergreen forests; lowlands; and lower hill-slopes; up to 1000 m. The current IUCN classification of Vulnerable (VU) underscores the importance of safeguarding its natural range. From its feeding habits to its nesting preferences, the Wallace Hawk Eagle is a testament to the intricate web of life. By studying species like the Wallace Hawk Eagle, we gain valuable insights into broader ecological trends.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
